page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the ARM64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 384 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance storage. Network connectivity is supported by a 50 Gbps baseline bandwidth, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU stress tests, memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks, and multi-threaded decompression tasks. Single-core performance is moderate, placing in the middle tier, which highlights a tradeoff favoring highly parallelized workloads over single-threaded execution speed. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier, reflecting efficient data handling and high throughput.

The ARMv9 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer high resource density and power efficiency, translating to qualitative cost advantages for scale-out workloads compared to traditional x86 architectures. The combination of high-capacity local NVMe storage, substantial memory bandwidth, and 96 physical cores makes this instance type suitable for high-throughput databases, large-scale web serving, multi-threaded data compression, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated physical ARM64 cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 384 GB of RAM, 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 50 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance remains moderate. The qualitative cost efficiency of the ARMv9 architecture makes it highly suitable for resource-dense deployments. This instance is optimized for high-throughput databases, multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and parallelized enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.24xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 96 physical cores, 384 GB RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD, optimized for high-throughput databases and parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.24xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 96 physical cores, 384 GB RAM, and 5700 GB NVMe SSD for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose platform featuring ninety-six dedicated cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and exceptional multi-core memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3
384 GB of system memory paired with 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory read/write bandwidth
Well-suited for high-throughput databases, Redis, and static web serving
